description: This schematic diagram illustrates the precipitation evolution mechanism in M2 steel (Fe-Mn-Al-C austenitic low-density steel containing Ti and V additions) during aging at 550°C. The left panel depicts the initial microstructure containing two (Ti, V)C carbides within the austenite matrix, where the color gradient from red center to green periphery indicates decreasing carbon content toward the carbide edges. The right panel shows the aged condition where κ-carbides (represented as gray rectangular blocks) heterogeneously nucleate and grow preferentially at the carbon-enriched edges of the (Ti, V)C carbides. The diagram visualizes the carbon sink mechanism: pre-existing (Ti, V)C carbides deplete carbon from the surrounding matrix, creating localized carbon gradients that drive κ-carbide precipitation at the carbide periphery rather than uniform distribution. This heterogeneous precipitation strategy explains how Ti/V additions optimize mechanical properties by maintaining work-hardening capacity while providing precipitation strengthening.
